questions.question: What is meant by development?
questions.answer: Development refers to improvement in the quality of life of people. It includes higher income, better education, good health facilities, equality and security.

questions.question: Explain the concept of developmental goals.
questions.answer: Developmental goals are the aspirations people have for a better life. These may include better income, job security, equal treatment and freedom. Different people have different goals.

questions.question: Why do developmental goals differ from person to person?
questions.answer: Developmental goals differ because people belong to different backgrounds, occupations and social groups. Their needs and priorities are different.

questions.question: What is per capita income? How is it calculated?
questions.answer: Per capita income is the average income of a country. It is calculated by dividing total national income by the total population.

questions.question: Why is per capita income not a complete measure of development?
questions.answer: Per capita income does not show income distribution, inequality or access to public facilities. It only shows average income.

questions.question: What is the Human Development Index (HDI)?
questions.answer: HDI is a composite index prepared by UNDP to measure development based on health, education and income indicators.

questions.question: Name the three main indicators used in HDI.
questions.answer: The three main indicators are life expectancy (health), years of schooling (education) and per capita income.

questions.question: What are public facilities? Give examples.
questions.answer: Public facilities are services provided by the government for public welfare. Examples include schools, hospitals, clean drinking water and transport.

questions.question: Why are public facilities important for development?
questions.answer: Public facilities improve living standards and ensure equal access to basic services for all citizens.

questions.question: What is sustainable development?
questions.answer: Sustainable development means using resources carefully so that future generations can also meet their needs.

questions.question: Why is overuse of groundwater a concern?
questions.answer: Overuse of groundwater leads to depletion of resources, affecting availability for future generations.

questions.question: What is national income?
questions.answer: National income is the total income earned by a country in a year from all economic activities.

questions.question: Explain the term infant mortality rate.
questions.answer: Infant mortality rate is the number of children who die before the age of one year per 1000 live births.

questions.question: Why is literacy rate important in development?
questions.answer: Literacy rate shows the level of education in a country and reflects social development.

questions.question: What are non-income indicators of development?
questions.answer: Non-income indicators include health, education, literacy rate and life expectancy.

questions.question: How can income and non-income goals conflict?
questions.answer: A factory may increase income but cause pollution, affecting health and environment.

questions.question: Why is equality important in development?
questions.answer: Equality ensures fair distribution of resources and opportunities among people.

questions.question: What is meant by average income?
questions.answer: Average income is the total income divided by the total population.

questions.question: Why does UNDP prepare HDI?
questions.answer: UNDP prepares HDI to compare countries based on overall human development.

questions.question: What is meant by economic development?
questions.answer: Economic development means growth in income along with improvement in living conditions.

questions.question: How does education contribute to development?
questions.answer: Education improves skills, employment opportunities and overall quality of life.

questions.question: Why is health an important development indicator?
questions.answer: Good health increases productivity and improves life expectancy.

questions.question: What are basic requirements for development?
questions.answer: Basic requirements include food, shelter, education, healthcare and employment.

questions.question: Why must development be inclusive?
questions.answer: Inclusive development ensures that benefits reach all sections of society.

questions.question: Explain the importance of sustainable resource use.
questions.answer: Sustainable use of resources prevents environmental damage and ensures availability for future generations.
